facts.statement: Utilitarianism is an ethical theory holding that the right action is the one that maximizes overall happiness or well-being.
questions: What is utilitarianism?
What ethical theory maximizes overall happiness?
What does utilitarianism say about right actions?
Who developed utilitarianism?
What ethical theory did Jeremy Bentham develop?
Who was the founder of utilitarianism?

facts.statement: John Stuart Mill refined utilitarianism in the nineteenth century.
questions: Who refined utilitarianism?
What did John Stuart Mill contribute to utilitarianism?
Which philosopher refined Bentham's utilitarianism?

facts.statement: Jeremy Bentham proposed measuring pleasure and pain to compare outcomes.
questions: How did Bentham propose to compare outcomes?
What method did Bentham use to evaluate actions?
What did Bentham measure in his utilitarian framework?

facts.statement: John Stuart Mill distinguished higher pleasures of the intellect from lower bodily pleasures.
questions: What distinction did Mill make about pleasures?
How did Mill categorize different types of pleasures?
What are higher and lower pleasures according to Mill?

facts.statement: Utilitarianism is a form of consequentialism.
questions: What type of ethical theory is utilitarianism?
Is utilitarianism a consequentialist theory?
What broader ethical framework does utilitarianism belong to?

facts.statement: Consequentialism judges actions by their results rather than intentions.
questions: How does consequentialism evaluate actions?
Does consequentialism focus on intentions or results?
What criterion does consequentialism use to judge actions?
